top_categories.workout_context: The Traka 200's Rocky Mediterranean gravel, Empordà plains gravel forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 126 miles.
Race-pace efforts that mimic The Traka 200's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before April.
8,694ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
69ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At mile 60 of 126, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
At The Traka 200, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At The Traka 200, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of The Traka 200. Train it until it feels boring.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on rocky Mediterranean gravel with forest singletrack. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
8,694ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 8,694ft of climbing on rocky Mediterranean gravel with forest singletrack does to your power file on race day.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 126 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for The Traka 200’s 126 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at The Traka 200. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Highly technical terrain at The Traka 200 demands practice on similar surfaces. Ride rocky mediterranean gravel with forest singletrack at race-day cadence weekly. Expect: rocky mediterranean gravel, empordà plains gravel, forest trails. Practice cornering, descending, and power delivery on unstable surfaces. Dial in tire pressure before race week — 5 PSI wrong costs you 15+ minutes over 126 miles.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Gravel Specific, Race Simulation, and VO2max to prepare you for 126 miles of rocky mediterranean gravel with forest singletrack in Girona, Catalonia, Spain.
